title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
The nullish coalescing operator (??) returns the right-hand side operand only when the left-hand side is null or undefined.

This is different from the logical OR (||) operator, which returns the right side for any falsy value (including 0, '', false, NaN).

Use ?? when 0, empty string, or false are valid values that you don't want to replace with a default.
